title: Antrophyum quadriseriatum
taxonomy: Antrophyum quadriseriatum is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is a type of plant that belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Pteridophyta, and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification places it within the broader category of vascular plants, which also includes conifers, cycads, and other types of ferns. The Pteridaceae family, to which Antrophyum quadriseriatum belongs, is a diverse group of ferns that are found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Within the Pteridaceae family, Antrophyum quadriseriatum is a unique species with its own set of characteristics and growth habits.

properties: Antrophyum quadriseriatum, also known as the quadrisegmented shield fern, is a plant species that has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. intro: Antrophyum quadriseriatum, commonly known as the four-striped lady fern, is a species of fern in the family Antrophyaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Antrophyum quadriseriatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Antrophyum quadriseriatum is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal ailments.
